name: Compurob.Game3d.Model.Jeep
comment:
elm-obj-file is used to decode various objects from the jeep model.

Body of the car, used for elm-physics simulation:

  - `convex-base_Base`
  - `convex-window_Window`

Positions of the wheels:

  - `front-right_Axis3d`
  - `rear-right_Axis3d`
  - `front-left_Axis3d`
  - `rear-left_Axis3d`

Used for rendering:

  - `chassis_Chassis`
  - `chassis-fender-left_fender-left`
  - `chassis-fender-right_fender-right`
  - `chassis-bumper_Bumper`
  - `chassis-cannister_Cannister`
  - `chassis-lamp-left_lamp-left`
  - `chassis-lamp-right_lamp-right`
  - `chassis-spare_Wheel`

Wheel, positioned at the origin:

  - `Wheel`

The Jeep model is courtesy of Kolja Wilcke <https://twitter.com/01k>

Adapted from <https://github.com/w0rm/elm-physics/blob/main/examples/src/RaycastCar/Jeep.elm>
by Andrey Kuzmin

@docs Jeep, load, settings, wheels

aliases.name: Jeep
aliases.comment: The Jeep model contains all ingredients for being able to render a Jeep.
aliases.type: { collider : List.List Physics.Shape.Shape, chassis : Scene3d.Mesh.Textured Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, chassisShadow : Scene3d.Mesh.Shadow Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, wheel : Scene3d.Mesh.Textured Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, wheelRadius : Length.Length, wheelWidth : Length.Length, wheelShadow : Scene3d.Mesh.Shadow Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, wheelAxes : List.List (Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates), material : Scene3d.Material.Textured Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates }

values.name: load
values.comment: Load a 3D Jeep model from an obj file url, and a texture image file url.
values.type: { texture : String.String, mesh : String.String } -> Task.Task String.String Compurob.Game3d.Model.Jeep.Jeep

values.name: settings
values.comment:
Jeep settings define the physical properties of the Jeep, e.g. its engine
force, braking force, wheel radius etc.

values.name: wheels
values.comment: Return a list of 4 wheels, configured for the Jeep.

name: Compurob.Game3d.Racing.V1
comment:
Adapted from <https://github.com/w0rm/elm-physics/blob/main/examples/src/RaycastCar.elm>
by Andrey Kuzmin.

  - `Game3d/Simulation/Car` — raycast vehicle simulation algorithm,
  - `Game3d/Model/Jeep` — load the 3d model using elm-obj-file.

Press arrow keys to drive, "b" to brake.

Try to add more obstacles to the demo by changing the initialWorld,
or tweak the settings in the `RayCastCar.Jeep` module!

@docs game, World, exampleWorld

aliases.name: World
aliases.comment: Representing the 3D world you can add things to like slopes and obstacles.

values.name: exampleWorld
values.comment: A world with gravity as on Earth, a floor and some obstacles.

values.name: game
values.comment:
Create a racing game providing car settings, including texture and 3d mesh
file url.

name: Compurob.Game3d.Simulation.Car
comment:
This is a complex example implementing raycast vehicle enspired by:

  - bullet3: <https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/blob/master/src/BulletDynamics/Vehicle/btRaycastVehicle.cpp>
  - cannon.js: <https://github.com/schteppe/cannon.js/blob/master/src/objects/RaycastVehicle.js>

This is a car with fake wheels, that shoots rays and applies
impulses to the car body where the rays hit the ground.

This allows to simulate suspension and results in smooth behavior.

Adapted from <https://github.com/w0rm/elm-physics/blob/main/examples/src/RaycastCar/Car.elm>
by Andrey Kuzmin

@docs Settings, Wheel, defaultWheel, simulate

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment:
The settings define the physical properties of the car,
such as what kind of suspension it has. Some expected
properties are still missing, like engine and breaking force
aliases.type: { downDirection : Direction3d.Direction3d Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, rightDirection : Direction3d.Direction3d Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, forwardDirection : Direction3d.Direction3d Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, suspensionRestLength : Length.Length, minSuspensionLength : Length.Length, maxSuspensionLength : Length.Length, radius : Length.Length, suspensionStiffness : Basics.Float, dampingCompression : Basics.Float, dampingRelaxation : Basics.Float, frictionSlip : Basics.Float, rollInfluence : Basics.Float, maxSuspensionForce : Force.Force, engineForce : Force.Force, brakingForce : Force.Force }

aliases.name: Wheel
aliases.comment:
Wheels are used to apply an impulse to contact points (preferably on the
road ;), so that the car they carry can move.
aliases.type: { chassisConnectionPoint : Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, axis : Axis3d.Axis3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.BodyCoordinates, steering : Angle.Angle, rotation : Angle.Angle, deltaRotation : Angle.Angle, suspensionImpulse : Quantity.Quantity Basics.Float (Quantity.Product Force.Newtons Duration.Seconds), suspensionLength : Length.Length, engineForce : Force.Force, brake : Force.Force, contact : Maybe.Maybe { point : Point3d.Point3d Length.Meters Physics.Coordinates.WorldCoordinates, normal : Direction3d.Direction3d Physics.Coordinates.WorldCoordinates, body : Physics.Body.Body id } }

values.name: simulate
values.type: Compurob.Game3d.Simulation.Car.Settings -> Duration.Duration -> { worldWithoutCar : Physics.World.World id, speeding : Basics.Float, steering : Basics.Float, braking : Basics.Bool } -> List.List (Compurob.Game3d.Simulation.Car.Wheel id) -> Physics.Body.Body id -> ( Physics.Body.Body id, List.List (Compurob.Game3d.Simulation.Car.Wheel id) )
